description: Prefix vs suffix self-clustering comparison across all verified systems
method: Auto-detected top-5 affix families (2-3 char, 2-20% coverage). Voynich uses standard EVA prefix families for prefix SC.
corpus_note: Leipzig languages use 20K sentences for this table. Voynich uses full corpus (31,608 tokens).

bucket_definitions.SYMM-HIGH: Both prefix and suffix SC > 1.1x, ratio between 0.80 and 1.25
bucket_definitions.SUFFIX-DOM: Suffix SC > 1.1x and ratio < 0.80, OR suffix > 1.1x and prefix < 1.1x
bucket_definitions.PREFIX-DOM: Prefix SC > 1.1x and ratio > 1.25, OR prefix > 1.1x and suffix < 1.1x

Ottoman Turkish.note: UD treebank (DUDU), 1782 sentences, 14th-20th century texts. Small corpus — wider CIs expected.

Swahili.note: Leipzig Wikipedia 100K. Despite rich bidirectional morphology (prefix AGR + suffix TAM), does not produce symmetric elevated self-clustering.

key_finding: Voynich is the only system in SYMM-HIGH. All natural languages with positive self-clustering are SUFFIX-DOM. No tested natural language clusters equally in both directions.

ablation_findings.arabic_al_stripped: Stripping al- prefix INCREASES SC (1.60x -> 2.41x). Arabic self-clustering is driven by root-pattern morphology, not just the definite article.
ablation_findings.voynich_family_ablation: QOK and OT drive self-clustering; CHEDY and AIIN drive transition rules. Two independent structural layers.
